name: MDCalc
description: The most widely used medical calculator and clinical decision support tool, with hundreds of validated scores and risk stratification tools.

name: LITFL – Life in the Fast Lane
description: Comprehensive emergency medicine and critical care education resource covering ECG library, toxicology, and clinical cases.

name: EMCrit
description: Emergency medicine and critical care education by Scott Weingart, focusing on resuscitation, airway management, and ICU-level ED care.

name: Radiopaedia
description: Collaborative radiology reference with thousands of cases, articles, and a comprehensive imaging encyclopedia.

name: REBEL EM
description: Evidence-based emergency medicine reviews critically appraising the latest research to guide clinical practice.

name: CoreEM
description: Core emergency medicine educational content covering key diagnoses, procedures, and clinical pearls.

name: OpenPediatrics
description: Global pediatric critical care education platform offering courses, simulations, and expert lectures for healthcare providers worldwide.

name: ClinCalc
description: Clinical calculators and drug dosing tools for healthcare professionals, including drug interaction checkers and statistical calculators.

name: Global RPh
description: Comprehensive pharmacist-curated drug dosing calculators, renal dosing guides, and clinical pharmacy references.

name: NephSIM
description: Interactive nephrology simulation platform for learning and practicing renal physiology and clinical decision-making.

name: PulmCCM
description: Evidence-based critical care and pulmonology updates summarising landmark trials and clinical guidelines for intensivists.

name: Taming the SRU
description: Emergency medicine education focused on point-of-care ultrasound, resuscitation, and managing the sickest ED patients.

name: EM Clerkship
description: Emergency medicine clerkship resource for medical students covering core presentations, procedures, and survival guides.

name: Pediatric Emergency Standards
description: Reference standards and clinical guidelines for pediatric emergency care, including weight-based dosing and resuscitation checklists.

name: Internet Book of Critical Care (IBCC)
description: Free online critical care textbook by Josh Farkas covering every ICU topic in depth with practical, evidence-based guidance.

name: StatPearls
description: Continuously updated, peer-reviewed medical reference available free via NCBI, covering thousands of clinical topics.

name: EBM Consult
description: Evidence-based medicine calculators, clinical criteria, and quick reference tools grounded in the primary literature.

name: Toronto Notes Online
description: Concise, comprehensive clinical review resource originally developed by University of Toronto medical students, widely used for board preparation.

name: Amboss (Free Tier)
description: Medical knowledge platform with a free tier providing access to a large library of clinical articles, Qbank previews, and drug information.

name: The NNT
description: Plain-language summaries of NNT (number needed to treat) and NNH for common therapies, making trial data accessible at the bedside.

name: Rational Clinical Examination (JAMA)
description: JAMA series reviewing the evidence behind physical examination findings and their diagnostic accuracy.

name: Teach Me Anatomy
description: Free, illustrated anatomy reference covering all body regions with clear diagrams and clinical correlations for students and clinicians.

name: Kenhub
description: Interactive anatomy learning platform with illustrated atlases, quizzes, and video tutorials (free tier available).

name: 3D Slicer
description: Open-source platform for medical image informatics, image processing, and 3D visualization used in research and clinical settings.

name: OpenMRS
description: Open-source electronic medical records platform designed for resource-constrained environments, powering health systems across Africa, Asia, and beyond.

name: BlockDocs
description: Free open-access reference for regional anesthesia and nerve blocks, with illustrated guides to ultrasound-guided and landmark techniques.

name: Coags
description: Practical anticoagulation and coagulation management tool for clinicians, covering dosing, reversal agents, and bridging decisions.

name: PEPT – Perioperative Electrophysiology Training Program
description: Welcome to the Perioperative Electrophysiology Training Program website. This website is designed to help anesthesiologists learn how to manage pacemakers and ICDs in the perioperative period. On this website, you will find the Credentialing Process we use at the MGH, the lectures that are required for this credentialing process, and much more.

name: BiliTool
description: BiliTool provides care recommendations for newborn infants with jaundice (hyperbilirubinemia) at risk for bilirubin encephalopathy and kernicterus.

name: CodeRunner – ACLS / PALS Companion
description: ACLS / PALS resuscitation protocol companion for trained healthcare providers to manage cardiac arrest and pediatric emergencies.
